informal: Prove that a group of even order contains an element of order $2 .$
lean:
theorem Artin_exercise_2_11_3 {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]
  (hG : Even (card G)) : ∃ x : G, orderOf x = 2
reasoning: The informal statement says a group of even order has an element of order 2. "Even order" implicitly means the group is finite with even cardinality, which is exactly captured by `[Fintype G]` together with `Even (card G)`. The conclusion `∃ x : G, orderOf x = 2` matches "contains an element of order 2" precisely. No extra hypotheses (like commutativity) are added, and no part of the claim is weakened. This is a faithful formalization.
